<?php namespace Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Relations; use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Collection as EloquentCollection; /** * @template TRelatedModel of \Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model * @template TDeclaringModel of \Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model * * @extends \Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Relations\HasOneOrMany<TRelatedModel, TDeclaringModel, \Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Collection<int, TRelatedModel>> */ class HasMany extends HasOneOrMany { /** * Convert the relationship to a "has one" relationship. * * @return \Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Relations\HasOne<TRelatedModel, TDeclaringModel> */ public function one() { return HasOne::noConstraints(fn () => tap( new HasOne( $this->getQuery(), $this->parent, $this->foreignKey, $this->localKey ), function ($hasOne) { if ($inverse = $this->getInverseRelationship()) { $hasOne->inverse($inverse); } } )); } /** @inheritDoc */ public function getResults() { return ! is_null($this->getParentKey()) ? $this->query->get() : $this->related->newCollection(); } /** @inheritDoc */ public function initRelation(array $models, $relation) { foreach ($models as $model) { $model->setRelation($relation, $this->related->newCollection()); } return $models; } /** @inheritDoc */ public function match(array $models, EloquentCollection $results, $relation) { return $this->matchMany($models, $results, $relation); } }
